qualifying_org_definition: Any bona fide nonprofit or community organization offering genuine volunteer work; no pre-approved state list currently required. NJ DFD is partnering with the Office of Volunteerism (HELPNJNOW portal) to build a voluntary registry, but org registration is not yet a gating condition. Per pending S3811 (committee-reported June 11, 2026, not yet enacted as of research date), a web-based registry would be established by Oct 1, 2026 — bill text does not make registration a prerequisite for hours to count.
verification_instrument: No standardized state form identified. The nj.gov ABAWD page instructs recipients to report volunteer activity via MyNJHelps.gov or their County Social Service Agency. Documentation standard appears to be a written statement (letter) from the volunteer organization confirming hours — analogous to an employer verification letter — submitted by the recipient to the county. County agencies may apply their own documentation templates. No dedicated CF 888-equivalent NJ state form found across multiple searches.
hours_formula_detail: NJ SNAP policy states explicitly: 'Working at least 80 hours a month, or an average of 20 hours a week.' This follows the federal default under 7 CFR 273.24. No workfare formula (allotment ÷ min wage) was found in NJ-specific policy.
accepts_outofstate_detail: No NJ SNAP policy source — including the nj.gov ABAWD page, the DFD federal-changes page, NJ Admin Code 10:87-10.20, or the pending S3811 — addresses the geographic location of the certifying volunteer organization. Under the federal default (7 CFR 273.24), any bona fide volunteer work counts regardless of organization location; absent an NJ-specific restriction, out-of-state 501(c)(3)s like Tended should be operationally accepted. Flagged 'silent' rather than 'yes' because no source affirmatively confirms it and county-level workers may exercise discretion.
geographic_waivers: As of February 1, 2026, only Camden City and Cape May County remain under an ABAWD time-limit waiver. All other NJ counties are fully enforced. The prior statewide waiver covering 20 counties expired January 31, 2026. OBBBA eliminated the waiver authority basis, so no new area waivers are expected.
submission_deadline: Recipients must report when weekly hours fall below the 20-hour average; changes should be reported within 10 days. Monthly ABAWD compliance is tracked at the county level — no statewide deadline for affirmative monthly hour submission identified. Interim Reporting Forms (IRF) are due mid-certification-period (typically every 6 months). Ongoing: changes reportable via MyNJHelps.gov, mail, or in-person at county agency.
notes:
NJ SNAP explicitly lists volunteer/in-kind work as qualifying toward the 80-hour ABAWD requirement with no identified mandatory EDP or county assignment prestep; however, no state-level verification form has been identified, county social service agencies administer SNAP locally with likely variation in how hours are documented, and the state DFD acknowledged it was still building out volunteer infrastructure as of early 2026. Tier 2 assigned due to county-level opacity and absence of a standardized state verification instrument — the pathway exists in policy but practical operability for an independent nonprofit like Tended is unclear without county-by-county confirmation. ABAWD enforcement expanded to nearly all NJ counties as of February 1, 2026 under HR1/OBBBA.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted adversarial verification pass with four fresh search angles plus direct page fetches targeting: (1) E&T state plan volunteer provisions, (2) approved-org/site-list existence, (3) work registration intake as a hidden prestep, (4) MOU/agreement requirements for nonprofits, and (5) pending S3811 legislation. Key findings: (a) CONFIRMED required_prestep=none — the nj.gov ABAWD page and E&T page both confirm E&T enrollment is 'completely voluntary' with no penalty for non-participation; volunteer work is listed as a qualifying activity alongside employment and self-employment with no mandatory case manager or EDP gating. (b) CONFIRMED no approved-site list currently in effect — DFD is 'in communication with the Office of Volunteerism to expand volunteer opportunities' (building, not enforcing). (c) IDENTIFIED pending S3811 (reported by Senate committee June 11, 2026, not yet enacted): would create a HELPNJNOW web-based volunteer registry by Oct 1, 2026, but bill text explicitly does not condition hour-counting on org registration — it is an infrastructure bill, not a gating mechanism. The HELPNJNOW portal (helpnjnow.communityos.org) is live but not yet in SNAP-compliance mode. (d) RESOLVED verification_instrument: no standardized state form exists; the documented mechanism is a written statement/letter from the volunteer org confirming hours, submitted by the recipient to their county agency — this is effectively self-reported with org attestation. (e) qualifying_org_definition remains open-ended at the state level; no exclusion criteria for nonprofits found. (f) remote_stance remains unknown — no state guidance found addressing whether remote/virtual volunteer tasks qualify. Confidence raised to 'medium' (from low): the general lane and no-prestep finding are confirmed across multiple official state sources, but no explicit policy manual or administrative directive text was found spelling out verification procedures, county variation in practice remains real and unresolved, and the pending S3811 creates forward uncertainty about whether a registry requirement will be added post-Oct 2026. Tier remains 2_hoops driven by county-administered variation in documentation — not a mandatory prestep — making Tended's practical county-level operability uncertain without direct county outreach.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) HOURS FORMULA: Confirmed federal_80 — nj.gov ABAWD page states verbatim '80 hours a month, or an average of 20 hours a week'; NJ Admin Code 10:87-10.20 similarly uses the 20-hr/week standard; no workfare (allotment ÷ min wage) formula found anywhere in NJ-specific SNAP policy. (2) OUT-OF-STATE NONPROFIT: Marked 'silent' — no NJ source (nj.gov, DFD, Admin Code, S3811) addresses whether the certifying volunteer org must be NJ-based; federal default (7 CFR 273.24) accepts any bona fide volunteer organization; operationally Tended should qualify but county workers may exercise undocumented discretion. (3) GEOGRAPHIC WAIVERS: As of February 1, 2026, only Camden City and Cape May County remain waived; prior 20-county waiver expired January 31, 2026; OBBBA eliminated the waiver authority basis. (4) SUBMISSION UX: MyNJHelps.gov (online portal) is the primary channel; mail and in-person county office also accepted; cadence is monthly compliance but formal submission is tied to IRF cycle (mid-certification, roughly every 6 months) and 10-day change reporting window; no statewide affirmative monthly deadline for volunteer hour submission found — county variation likely. (5) FORM FIELDS: No standardized NJ state form exists; fields inferred from general employment verification letter standards applicable to NJ county agencies — org name/address/phone/signature, supervisor name, recipient name, total hours, hours by period, activity description, and start/end dates are the baseline expectation; case number and DOB not confirmed as required by any NJ source.
